page: OVHcloud t2-le-90 is a GPU-accelerated server instance within the Cloud GPU family, utilizing KVM virtualization. The hardware configuration features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 6226R processor operating at 2.9 GHz, delivering 30 dedicated physical cores and 30 vCPUs with no hyperthreading. Memory resources consist of 90.0 GB of RAM, providing 3.0 GB per core. For graphics and parallel processing acceleration, the server is equipped with two NVIDIA Volta V100S GPUs, providing a combined total of 64 GB VRAM. Local storage is provided via a 500 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 4 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results highlight the server's capabilities, particularly in machine learning workloads. LLM inference speeds are notable, achieving top-tier performance with 17320.17 tokens per second for prompt processing on a small 135M model, and 18.65 tokens per second for text generation on a large 70B model. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is strong, registering at 228.36 GB/sec. Standard CPU benchmarks show average performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 10904.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 36761.32. Redis SET operations average 44.21 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput reaches 23.04 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the t2-le-90 offers a dense allocation of dedicated physical cores and dual enterprise-grade GPUs, making it a viable option for compute-heavy parallel tasks without the overhead of newer-generation hardware premiums. This server is suited for workloads such as large language model inference, deep learning training, scientific simulations, and multi-threaded data decompression.
description: OVHcloud t2-le-90 is a dedicated GPU-accelerated server designed for parallel computing and machine learning workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6226R processor with 30 dedicated physical cores and 30 vCPUs running at 2.9 GHz, it features 90.0 GB of RAM and a 500 GB NVMe SSD. The primary performance driver is its dual NVIDIA Volta V100S GPUs, which deliver a total of 64 GB VRAM. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier efficiency in LLM inference, achieving 151.96 tokens per second for prompt processing on a large 70B model. It also shows strong multi-threaded decompression capabilities, with bzip3 reaching 228.36 GB/sec. This hardware profile makes the instance highly suitable for deep learning, scientific computing, and high-throughput data processing tasks.
og_description: OVHcloud t2-le-90 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 30 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 90 GB RAM, a 500 GB NVMe SSD, and dual NVIDIA Volta V100S GPUs with 64 GB total VRAM for parallel computing workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud t2-le-90 is a GPU-accelerated server with 30 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 90 GB RAM, and dual NVIDIA V100S GPUs for machine learning workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-GPU server featuring thirty physical cores and NVMe storage, optimized for high-throughput parallel processing and machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA Volta V100S GPUs with 64 GB total VRAM
30 dedicated Intel Xeon 6226R physical cores at 2.9 GHz
90 GB RAM and 500 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier LLM inference performance across small, medium, and large models
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Suited for deep learning, scientific simulations, and parallel data processing
